channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Los Angeles Lakers
channel.description: Los Angeles Lakers trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: LeBron James sincerely unsure about whether he wants to continue playing
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] League sources continue to say James is sincerely unsure about whether he wants to continue playing. A return to the Lakers is a possibility, though he’s sure to have other suitors in free agency, especially if he takes a sizable pay cut from the $52.6 million he made this season. New York Times

item.title: James is still uncertain about his future.
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Sources close to James told the California Post that James is still uncertain about his future. He’s going to take some time and evaluate where he’s at mentally, physically and emotionally. He echoed that after the Lakers were swept out of the playoffs by the Thunder with a 115-110 loss in Game 4 on Monday. New York Post

item.title: Byron Scott, a former star for them during the …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] The Lakers aren't far from being a championship-caliber team, but they may need to make a couple of significant changes this offseason in order to get to that level. Byron Scott, a former star for them during the Showtime era, feels one of those changes has to be telling James to hit the road. “You getting paid $50 million,” Scott said. “You’re 41, 42, 43 years old, right? You got a three-headed monster like you’re talking about. The Lakers, if you want to move on and be a c

item.title: No matter what people might think, what might have …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Where do you come out on that subject? LeBron or Mike?” Jerry Stackhouse: “I mean, from some skill standpoints, he’s probably better. He’s a better ball handler than Michael Jordan was. I think he’s a better facilitator than Michael Jordan was. Was he a better scorer than Michael Jordan? No. Was he a better clutch performer? No. Michael Jordan went to the Finals six times, won six championships, six Finals MVPs. So, to me, he’s always going to be the GOAT in my book. No matt

item.title: Jerry Stackhouse: “Yeah, he’s probably right behind …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Jim Rome: “I’m not going to argue with that point. I’m an MJ guy. So where do you put Bron? If Kareem’s number two, where’s Bron go?” Jerry Stackhouse: “Yeah, he’s probably right behind there. People are probably going to have Kobe in there, too. Like, where’s Shaq? There are a whole lot of different ways you could go there. But I don’t think you can put LeBron lower than three, just with the longevity, the body of work, and to have this sustainable greatness for so long. Yo

item.title: Dillon Brooks on LeBron James: I don't think it's his last year, I think he has one more in him
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] “I don’t think it’s his last year,” Suns forward Dillon Brooks, who was in attendance, told Yahoo Sports. “I think he got one more in him. I watched him when I was in the NBA, when I was a kid in high school. He’s got a phenomenal career and battled everybody and done it at a high level throughout. It’s been amazing to watch overall, a pleasure to battle against him.” Yahoo! Sports

item.title: Lakers want LeBron James to return
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Dave McMenamin: Rob Pelinka says the Lakers want to "honor" LeBron James by giving him the time to make a decision on his future, but want him back if he wants to come back. He says the team also wants Austin Reaves to return and have expressed that sentiment to Reaves' representatives. x.com

item.title: "We probably haven't seen a player that has honored …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Rob Pelinka: Rob Pelinka on LeBron: "We probably haven't seen a player that has honored the game to the extent that he's honored the game. He's given so much to his teammates, to this organization. The thing we want to do more than anything else is to honor him back" x.com

item.title: Rob Pelinka: Lakers have made it 'abundantly clear' that they want to keep Austin Reaves
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Dan Woike: Pelinka says both sides have made it “abaduntely clear” that they want to work to keep Austin Reaves with the Lakers. x.com

item.title: "He started his journey here as a Laker and has made …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Law Murray: Rob Pelinka on Austin Reaves: "He started his journey here as a Laker and has made it very clear to us that he wants his journey to continue as a Laker. And we feel the same way. We want his odyssey to continue to unfold in the purple and gold." x.com

item.title: "Any team, including ours, would love to have LeBron …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Law Murray: Rob Pelinka: "Any team, including ours, would love to have LeBron James on their roster" x.com

item.title: "He was phenomenal this year as a teammate, and a …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Oh No He Didn't: JJ Redick on Luka Doncic: "He was phenomenal this year as a teammate, and a leader...the guy had a tier 1 season relative to the rest of the league" x.com

item.title: Rob Pelinka says the Lakers will be hiring a pair of …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Dan Woike: Rob Pelinka says the Lakers will be hiring a pair of assistant general managers. x.com

item.title: Pelinka said he thought JJ Redick dealt with having …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Dan Woike: Rob Pelinka said he thought JJ Redick dealt with having “three quarterbacks” and praised the sacrifice and humility of the Lakers’ on-court leaders to figure it out. x.com

item.title: “It's not hey, ‘I want X, Y and Z,’ so we're in …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Benjamin Royer: I asked Rob Pelinka about his communication with Luka Doncic over offseason plans: “It's not hey, ‘I want X, Y and Z,’ so we're in constant communication with him and his team. I plan on seeing him before he takes off to go home and spend some time with his daughters.” x.com

item.title: Rob Pelinka calls the Lakers' 16-2 run from late …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Dave McMenamin: Rob Pelinka calls the Lakers' 16-2 run from late February through the end of March a "strong data point and proof of concept" that will inform the way the franchise puts together the roster for next season. x.com

item.title: “Whether that's internal development, draft, trade, …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Benjamin Royer: I asked JJ Redick about roster construction and depth — and what he’d like to see: “Whether that's internal development, draft, trade, free agency, whatever it is, we need to build depth beyond just an eight or nine-man rotation, because you're naturally going to have injuries.” x.com

item.title: Rob Pelinka says the Lakers are in "constant …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Dave McMenamin: Rob Pelinka says the Lakers are in "constant communication" with Luka Doncic and calls him an "incredible partner" when discussing team building preferences. x.com

item.title: Lakers GM Rob Pelinka: "In terms of the season, lots …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Mark Medina: Lakers GM Rob Pelinka: "In terms of the season, lots to be proud of in terms of back-to-win 50-win seasons, but nothing to be satisfied with." x.com

item.title: Luka Doncic wants Lakers to re-sign Austin Reaves
item.description: Dončić has made it clear to the Lakers that he would like to continue playing with Reaves, according to league sources. Beyond their close friendship, Dončić believes in Reaves as a long-term piece next to him. Multiple league sources said that belief was best illustrated when Dončić told people within the organization that he wouldn’t want Reaves included in any potential trade packages for Milwaukee’s Giannis Antetokounmpo. Dončić, those sources say, would strongly prefer a team constr

item.title: Other teams that are known to have some interest in …
item.description: Other teams that are known to have some interest in Reaves — like Utah and Atlanta, per league sources — would need to make roster moves to find the necessary space. League sources say winning will be a significant factor in Reaves’ thinking.

item.title: Rival executives predicted Reaves could command $40 …
item.description: While team and league sources believe that the Lakers and Reaves will eventually land on a contract that makes sense, the door for Reaves to walk is open should he get a big offer elsewhere. Rival executives predicted Reaves could command $40 million a season due to a combination of his play, his age and, perhaps most importantly, a free-agent class completely devoid of players as productive as him in their prime.

item.title: Here’s why LeBron James saying he’s not sure about his …
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: Here’s why LeBron James saying he’s not sure about his future is the most accurate answer he could give. Beyond the obvious decision of whether he still wants to put the work in to play at the level he does, it’s also a matter of what options he has. A lot will happen between now and free agency. The draft, trades, etc. The whole league could look different depending on who wins this year and whether players like Giannis Antetokounmpo are traded before the draft/free agency. So

item.title: Gary Payton: LeBron James is coming to Golden State if he leaves Lakers
item.description: NBA Courtside: Gary Payton on LeBrons future: “There’s a good shot if he leaves, he’s going to Golden State. I’m telling you he’s coming to Golden State if he leaves” (Via @NightcapShow_)

item.title: Those priorities remain, according to league sources. …
item.description: Those priorities remain, according to league sources. There is a desire for better center play, true lob threats who mimic the skills of Daniel Gafford and Dereck Lively II (with cleaner injury histories). There is a desire for true two-way defensive players, wings with athleticism and energy on the defensive end and a sweet shooting stroke on the offensive side of the ball. And there’s a desire to lock in a long-term secondary playmaker next to Dončić.

item.title: Smart, whom Dončić recruited in free agency, could opt …
item.description: Ayton, who certainly played to his $8.1 million contract this season, had a strong first-round series against Houston All-Star Alperen Şengün. He could opt out to try to find a bigger payday with the Lakers or elsewhere. Smart, whom Dončić recruited in free agency, could opt out of his deal and seek a longer-term contract. The Lakers have interest in retaining him.

item.title: Asking the NBA’s all-time leading scorer, a player …
item.description: Asking the NBA’s all-time leading scorer, a player still impossibly effective at 41 years old, to change his game was a delicate moment. In his meetings with Dončić and Reaves, Redick repeatedly discussed “empathy,” team sources said. While James publicly would say he’d do whatever necessary to help the Lakers win, the team’s stakeholders were still unsure about how he’d handle the new hierarchy. Making this work, they thought, would be difficult.

item.title: “We just don’t have enough good players,” one locker …
item.description: The Lakers finished 11 games behind the Thunder in the regular season. Then they finished the playoffs 64 points worse than them in a four-game sweep. Once again, the Thunder were too good — too versatile, too deep, too balanced. “We just don’t have enough good players,” one locker room source lamented.

item.title: The explanation I’ve heard is Kleber was in as a …
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: The explanation I’ve heard is Kleber was in as a screener. Kennard a decoy (third option). AR or Bron was getting the shot. Smart was the passer. Kleber is a good screener and good with execution. Hence, he was in there over Rui. Neither was getting the ball. Kennard in there over Rui because of the gravity he creates.

item.title: LeBron James is now 0-11 in game-tying/go-ahead FG in …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: LeBron James is now 0-11 in game-tying/go-ahead FG in closeout, elimination, or NBA Finals games (last 24 seconds). (via WarriorsMuse) #NBA

item.title: Austin Reaves on free agency: “I don’t think about …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Lakers guard Austin Reaves on free agency: “I don’t think about much. I take life day by day. I’m blessed to have an opportunity to play for this organization. I play a kid’s game. I make good money.”

item.title: LeBron James: 'There's nothing I need to show... I've done it all, seen it all'
item.description: Law Murray: Asked LeBron James if there are things he hasn't done before that he would like to show if he continues playing "... No. yeah...no. There's nothing I need to show... I've done it all, seen it all"

item.title: The process to me has always been so much more …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: LeBron James on basketball as a 41-year-old: "The love of the game is always there. I don't think it ever goes away. ... But the process to me has always been so much more important. Because if I fell out of love with the process, then I'd probably fall out of love with the game"

item.title: "Winning a championship, 2020 stands at the top. That …
item.description: Lakers Daily: LeBron on bringing the Lakers back to relevance: "Winning a championship, 2020 stands at the top. That was the reason why I came here, to restore that level of play and restore this franchise back to what it was known for."

item.title: "I guess when you get like over 40 you start …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Lebron: "I guess when you get like over 40 you start forgetting shit that happened. So I don't even remember many of the last times walking off the court" 🤣

item.title: LeBron James: "None of us know what the future …
item.description: Law Murray: LeBron James: "None of us know what the future holds... and I don't either. Like I said, I'll take time to recalibrate and see what's best for my future"

item.title: LeBron James: "I have never been a third option in my …
item.description: ‪Benjamin Royer: LeBron James: "I have never been a third option in my life, ... So to be able to thrive in that role for that period of time, and then have to step back into the role ... and then my teammates allow me to like lead them under extreme circumstances, that was pretty cool for me."

item.title: LeBron James on finishing up season 23: "Listen, s---, …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: LeBron James on finishing up season 23: "Listen, s---, I left everything I could out on the floor. ... I'm not looking at my year as a disappointment, that's for damn sure."

item.title: "I want to spend time with my daughters. And that's …
item.description: Benjamin Royer: Luka Doncic says the first thing on his mind for the offseason is family: "I want to spend time with my daughters. And that's probably the only thing that is on my mind right now."

item.title: “It’s amazing what he’s doing out there at this age. …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Shai on playing against 41-year-old LeBron James in a series: “It’s amazing what he’s doing out there at this age. It’s hard to put into words. He was impressive out there. I’m not sure we’ll see anything like that. It was super fun. Glad I got to get four wins.”

item.title: LeBron James: 'I don't know what the future holds for me'
item.description: Benjamin Royer: LeBron James on his future: "I don't know what the future holds for me, obviously, as it stands right now."

item.title: Luka on if he'll try to recruit Lebron, Austin, Ayton …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Luka on if he'll try to recruit Lebron, Austin, Ayton and Smart this summer: "We'll see. Can't tell you nothing"

item.title: “I feel very comfortable. … I like being a Laker” - …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: “I feel very comfortable. … I like being a Laker” - Luka Doncic on his first full season in Los Angeles

item.title: Ui Hachimura relatable content: "I'm not a good …
item.description: ‪Law Murray 📘‬: Rui Hachimura relatable content: "I'm not a good negotiator. I put all that on my agents"

item.title: Luka Doncic will not play for Slovenia this summer
item.description: Dan Woike: Luka Dončić says he will not play for Slovenia this summer, as he focuses on securing joint custody of his daughters.

item.title: Luka Doncic: "I know some people wanted me back, but …
item.description: Benjamin Royer: Luka Doncic: "I know some people wanted me back, but obviously I wasn't close to clearing."

item.title: Luka Doncic says that he will likely be cleared for …
item.description: Law Murray: Luka Doncic says that he will likely be cleared for contact next week

item.title: Luka Doncic: 'If I could be out there, I would be'
item.description: ‪ ‪‪Edwin Garcia‬: "If I could be out there, I would be," Luka Dončić said. He also mentioned there were a lot of false rumors about his injury on the internet, but those people didn't see his MRIs.

item.title: Austin: "I'm blessed to have an opportunity to play …
item.description: Lakers Daily: Austin: "I'm blessed to have an opportunity to play for this organization. ... I don't think about what I'm really gonna do in the future"

item.title: Luke Kennard on his impending free agency says it's …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: Luke Kennard on his impending free agency says it's been an honor to play for the Lakers' organization and his goal, heading into Year 10, is to win. He says if he does not re-sign in L.A., he will always appreciate his time as a Laker.

item.title: The spotlight is on and, and you feel it but like for …
item.description: Benjamin Royer: Luke Kennard on playing for the Lakers in LA: "It feels like the biggest stage in basketball, like it's you're in the NBA, you're playing for the Los Angeles Lakers; the spotlight is on and, and you feel it but like for me, like I love it like I love being a part of it."

item.title: Luke Kennard, referring to the Lakers' March run: "Our …
item.description: Benjamin Royer: Luke Kennard, referring to the Lakers' March run: "Our group was really close and connected through it all. And you don't always get that in the NBA, and I think just kind of shows how special this group is."

item.title: Deandre Ayton says he hasn't thought about his player …
item.description: Khobi Price: Deandre Ayton says he hasn't thought about his player option for 2026-27. Says he trusts his agents. "I love it here regardless."

item.title: LeBron James recorded his 20th career 20-point …
item.description: ESPN Insights: LeBron James recorded his 20th career 20-point double-double when facing elimination, tying Wilt Chamberlain for most in NBA history 🔥

item.title: LeBron James this postseason has outscored all players 41 or older in NBA playoff history combined
item.description: Tom Haberstroh: PLAYERS AGE 41 OR OLDER NBA Playoff History LeBron James (this season) ........ 232 points All non-LeBron players (ever) ..... 212 points (via @Stathead)

item.title: LeBron James (this season) ........ 73 assists All …
item.description: Tom Haberstroh: PLAYERS AGE 41 OR OLDER NBA Playoff History LeBron James (this season) ........ 73 assists All non-LeBron players (ever) ..... 22 assists (via @Stathead)

item.title: Austin Reaves on playing with LeBron James again: 'It would mean the world to me'
item.description: Benjamin Royer: Austin Reaves on the potential of continuing to play with LeBron James: "It would mean the world to me. I don't know anything different. My rookie year, I had no idea what the hell was going on, and he basically took me under his wing and (has) given me every opportunity."

item.title: Austin Reaves on LeBron James: "It's been fun [playing …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: Austin Reaves on LeBron James: "It's been fun [playing with him]. I hope I can continue to do that"

item.title: First time LeBron James has been swept in either of …
item.description: ‪Justin Russo‬: This was the first time LeBron James has been swept in either of the first two rounds of the postseason. The only times he was swept before this were in the Finals (2007 & 2018) and the Conference Finals (2023).

item.title: "'Helluva shot. Keep your head up.'" Austin Reaves …
item.description: Dan Woike: "'Helluva shot. Keep your head up.'" Austin Reaves said LeBron James found him after missed the game-tying shot and encouraged him. "...He's meant a lot to my career."

item.title: Austin Reaves on OKC: "They're really f---ing good."
item.description: Dave McMenamin: Austin Reaves on OKC: "They're really f---ing good."

item.title: Austin Reaves: "I've been around the NBA for five …
item.description: Benjamin Royer: Austin Reaves: "I've been around the NBA for five years now. I know a lot of teams would have given up ..., and that wasn't the case with this team. It's been fun to go to work every single day."

item.title: JJ Redick doesn't believe in moral victories, he says
item.description: Benjamin Royer: JJ Redick doesn't believe in moral victories, he says. "I don't know who the f came up with it."

item.title: I always look like at as season as a living organism …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: JJ Redick: "I told the team this, is my 17th year being part of an NBA team ... I always look like at as season as a living organism ... when the season ends, that organism is dead and that organism will never live again. ... I didn't want our season to end."

item.title: JJ Redick on what the gap is between the Lakers and …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: JJ Redick on what the gap is between the Lakers and being a championship team: "That's what we got to figure out this offseason ... [But] I take a lot of pride in our players ... and what we turned out to be"

item.title: "I care as much about this as anything. No, I love my …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "I care as much about this as anything. No, I love my wife and kids." 😅 —JJ Redick after the Thunder ended the Lakers' season

item.title: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having …
item.description: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having words for OKC after foul 😂

item.title: Rui Hachimura: — Highest 3P% in NBA playoff history
item.description: StatMuse: Rui Hachimura: — Highest 3P% in NBA playoff history — Leading Lakers in threes this playoffs — Led Lakers in threes tonight Wasn't on the floor for the game-tying three.

item.title: LeBron this playoffs: — 23.2 PPG
item.description: StatMuse: LeBron this playoffs: — 23.2 PPG — 6.7 RPG — 7.3 APG — Led Lakers in points — Led Lakers in assists Reminder: he's 41 years old.

item.title: Free throw attempts this series: 70 — Lakers
item.description: StatMuse: Free throw attempts this series: 70 — Lakers 60 — Thunder

item.title: LeBron James this season: — Oldest player with 20/5/5 …
item.description: StatMuse: LeBron James this season: — Oldest player with 20/5/5 season — Oldest player with a triple-double — Oldest player with a 25-point game — Oldest player with a 30-point game — Oldest player with a 35-point game — Oldest player with a 10-assist game — Top 3 in PTS, REB, AST on a playoff team

item.title: Thunder sweep Lakers
item.description: Law Murray 📘‬: The Thunder went 8-0 against the Los Angeles Lakers and swept them in the semifinals Final score: Thunder 115, Lakers 110 Thunder are 8-0 in playoffs Lakers season ends

item.title: The Lakers lost 115-110 against the Thunder ending …
item.description: ‪Edwin Garcia‬: The Lakers lost 115-110 against the Thunder ending their season. OKC just outexecuted LA down the stretch.

item.title: “My time in Seattle? Bittersweet. I did not get along …
item.description: Kendall has never been one to shy away from the truth, especially when it comes to his time in Seattle playing for Hall of Famer George Karl. If you thought time healed those wounds, think again. Kendall stayed candid: “My time in Seattle? Bittersweet. I did not get along with George Karl. But I loved my teammates; I loved playing with them. We won 63 games. We had a whip. Okay? I thought we were going to win the NBA Championship the first year that I was there. But, unfortunately we got beat by

item.title: Shams Charania: Giannis Antetokounmpo is expected to tell the Bucks 'no' when asked to commit long-term
item.description: Antetokounmpo has one guaranteed year remaining on his contract, giving him considerable leverage over his next destination. The Miami Heat, Golden State Warriors, Los Angeles Lakers, Minnesota Timberwolves and Cleveland Cavaliers are among the teams expected to pursue him. "Any conversation that the Bucks are going to have with Giannis Antetokounmpo, from my understanding, is going to be a very simple one: 'Do you want to stay here long-term and commit long-term in June?'" Charania said on the

item.title: “I would say all indicators say that he will continue …
item.description: Speaking on ESPN’s NBA Today, McMenamin said “all indicators” point toward James continuing his NBA career beyond this season — though uncertainty remains about whether that future will still be with the Los Angeles Lakers. “This is the first time in his Laker career, eight years in, we’ll be going into a summer without a contract option with the Lakers, fully unrestricted free agent,” McMenamin said. “I would say all indicators say that he will continue playing NBA basketball, but the question

item.title: “I think, if we all know LeBron James, he’s gonna take …
item.description: Wade does not know for sure whether James plans to retire at the end of the 2025-26 season if the Los Angeles Lakers bow out to the Oklahoma City Thunder, or whether James wants to stay in L.A. or take his talents to another team for another year. But he believes the James he knows will need some time to think it over. “That’s the question,” Wade, who made four consecutive NBA Finals and won two championships alongside James, said during the Game 4 broadcast. “I think, if we all know LeBron Jame

item.title: “I don’t think no one knows,” Wade said. “I think one …
item.description: Wade acknowledged he does not have any definitive answers about James’ future, though he said “history shows that Mr. James ain’t taking a lot of discounts.” “I don’t think no one knows,” Wade said. “I think one of the things that him and his entire team have been great at is they hold their cards close to their chest. Decisions are made by LeBron, and they all respect it, and they wait on him to decide what he wants to do. So I don’t think none of us can go there until he makes the decision for

item.title: Front office maintaining asking price of "a young …
item.description: Underdog NBA: Latest on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade front, per @ShamsCharania : - Front office maintaining asking price of "a young blue-chip talent and/or a surplus of draft picks" - Bucks also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Giannis to stay once again - Wolves, Cavs, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers pursued Giannis at the trade deadline

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander on opponents complaining to refs about way Thunder are officiated: It's just distractions off the court
item.description: It's not a laughing matter to the Lakers and a lot of others around the league. As a potential dynasty rises in Oklahoma City, there are frequent complaints by competitors about the Thunder getting a friendly whistle. "I just think it's all distractions away from the court," Gilgeous-Alexander told ESPN. "That's how I see it. Whether they're right or wrong, the refs have made a call, and unless you challenge it, they're not going to change it. "It's just another thing I can't control, so I reall

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: Largest point differential during a seven-game win …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Largest point differential during a seven-game win streak in the playoffs in NBA history: 1. 2026 Knicks: +185 . . . . . . . . . . . . 2. 1985 Lakers: +133 3. 2017 Cavs: +131 4. 2010 Magic: +129 5. 2026 Thunder: +128 6. 1986 Celtics: +127

item.title: The Lakers initially assumed he would be out until the …
item.description: Initially doctors told Reaves he would be out 4-6 weeks with the injury, but probably on the longer end of that timeline. The Lakers initially assumed he would be out until the conference finals, team sources told ESPN, but Reaves was determined to get back for at least some of the Lakers' playoff run. "I left my house every day around 7:30 in the morning to get treatment and didn't come home until about 8 at night," Reaves told ESPN. "I was going crazy trying to get back. ... I was in that hype

item.title: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in …
item.description: ‪NBA‬: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in the play-by-play era (1997-98) to record 300+ fourth quarter PTS in the postseason before turning 25! Joining: Kobe Bryant (625 PTS) LeBron James (493 PTS) Jayson Tatum (388 PTS) Kevin Durant (388 PTS) Dwyane Wade (357 PTS)

item.title: Tonight was Anthony Edwards’ 18th postseason game with …
item.description: ‪NBA‬: Tonight was Anthony Edwards’ 18th postseason game with 30+ points, tying Dwyane Wade for the fourth-most by a player before turning 25. Trailing only... LeBron James (30) Kobe Bryant (26) Kevin Durant (22)

item.title: Luka Doncic (left hamstring strain) remains out for …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Luka Doncic (left hamstring strain) remains out for Game 4 against OKC tomorrow

item.title: Jalen Williams (left hamstring strain) remains out for …
item.description: Justin Martinez: OKC's Jalen Williams (left hamstring strain) remains out for Game 4 against the Los Angeles Lakers tomorrow.

item.title: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with …
item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with multiple 30-point series-clinching wins in a single postseason. They join the 2025 Thunder, 2008 Celtics, and 1987 Lakers. All 3 of those teams went on to win the NBA title.

item.title: Rui Hachimura on the energy in today's practice, with …
item.description: Khobi Price: Rui Hachimura on the energy in today's practice, with the Lakers aware of the challenge they have being down 0-3 to the Thunder: "It's tough. They are championship [team] for a reason. We focus on a lot on Shai but I think we talk about more...we should have talked about more other guys. They're killing us. Especially when Shai is off the court, we're really losing those minutes. We talked about that today, how we're gonna guard them as a team. We feel good about it tomorrow."

item.title: Should there be a Game 5 the Lakers vs. Thunder will …
item.description: Rylan Stiles: Should there be a Game 5 the Lakers vs. Thunder will tip off at 8:30 PM CT Wednesday.

item.title: Khobi Price: JJ Redick when asked about this sequence …
item.description: Khobi Price: JJ Redick when asked about this sequence from Deandre Ayton: "I’ve been around DA for a season now. I know how special he is for our group to succeed. And I told him this morning, I said to him in the Houston series at one point he was the big part of the reason we were winning the series. He was a big part of the reason we won the series. I believe in him and he’s going to help us win tomorrow."

item.title: “I mean, just thinking about it, I prayed for moments …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: “I mean, just thinking about it, I prayed for moments like these and worked for moments like these, so just to get that opportunity has been special and go out there and do what I can for the guys” - Adou Thiero on going from out of the rotation to grabbing 8 reb in 13 mins in G3

item.title: "I mean, being on the Lakers, you feel like your …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: "I mean, being on the Lakers, you feel like your back’s against the wall every game. So I think the whole season has prepared us to be in a position where we're in …” - JJ Redick on if the adversity LAL has faced this season preps them for this OKC challenge

item.title: Deandre Ayton’s signature soft touch around the basket …
item.description: Deandre Ayton’s signature soft touch around the basket has suddenly escaped him. After shooting 60.4% from the field during the first round and a career-best 67.1% during the regular season, Ayton is shooting 39.3% (11 for 28) against the Thunder. He made just three of 11 shots in the restricted area during the first two games. The Lakers needed Ayton to thrive during this series against the double-big lineup of Chet Holmgren and Isaiah Hartenstein. Some of Ayton’s shooting struggles have depend

item.title: Austin Reaves, sigh, has clearly been unable to …
item.description: Austin Reaves, sigh, has clearly been unable to overcome his oblique injury and playoff demons. He was lousy again Saturday with five baskets and five turnovers and a bunch of guff for the officials. This left the major hauling to James, who’s not officially hurt, but here’s guessing his whole body is hurting. He’s not officially exhausted, but here’s guessing his whole body is tired. He’s not officially a senior citizen, but in NBA terms he’s definitely a senior citizen and during the most impo

item.title: If the Warriors do somehow get their hands on another …
item.description: Another reason to trust Kerr: If the Warriors do somehow get their hands on another star — and you can bet a Lacob-run franchise will try — then Golden State will require Kerr’s greatest strength of maximizing excellence. In the alternate universe where the Warriors land Giannis Antetokounmpo or Kawhi Leonard or LeBron James, Kerr is the coach you’d want. Go watch “Court of Gold” on Netflix and see why.

item.title: Pratt was spotted in the fourth quarter sitting in …
item.description: Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt made an appearance at Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals between the Oklahoma City Thunder and the Lakers on Saturday night at Crypto.com Arena. Pratt was spotted in the fourth quarter sitting in former owner Jeanie Buss’ second-row seats behind the court.

item.title: "I wish we had more time with those guys all in the …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: JJ Redick on the big 3: "I wish we had more time with those guys all in the lineup. We were just beginning to see what that could look like when Luka and AR got hurt and that's sort of the disappointing part because I think we all felt that excitement and that belief that we were beginning to be a championship level team and would have loved to have seen that group that was finishing the regular season be able to play healthy in the playoffs"
